taxonomy: Listrostachys virgula is a species within the family Bromeliaceae. This family is commonly known as the pineapple family, encompassing around 57 genera and approximately 3,000 species of tropical plants. The genus Listrostachys is a small group of terrestrial bromeliads that are primarily found in South America. As a member of the Bromeliaceae family, Listrostachys virgula is part of the order Poales. The classification of Listrostachys virgula is also part of the larger classification of monocotyledonous flowering plants.

intro: Listrostachys virgula, also known as the "Verguinho" or "Verguinha", is a plant species native to the tropical regions of South America, belonging to the Acanthaceae family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, making it useful in the treatment of various health conditions, such as wounds, infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Listrostachys virgula include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, Listrostachys virgula is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in the production of topical creams and ointments for wound care.
